design_name: Flanged shaft coupling with two disc flanges and a central hub cylinder, featuring a center bore, keyway, and six bolt holes through the flanges.
prompt: A flanged shaft coupling standing along the Z axis, centered at the origin in X and Y. The coupling consists of three coaxial cylindrical sections: a bottom flange disc 50mm in diameter and 10mm thick (Z=0 to Z=10), a central hub cylinder 28mm in diameter and 40mm long (Z=10 to Z=50), and a top flange disc 50mm in diameter and 10mm thick (Z=50 to Z=60). The total length is 60mm. Six bolt holes 6.5mm in diameter are equally spaced on a 38mm pitch circle diameter (19mm radius), computed at 0, 60, 120, 180, 240, and 300 degrees from the +X axis. The bolt holes pass through the full 60mm length but only cut the flanges since the hub is narrower. A 14mm diameter center bore runs the full 60mm length. A rectangular keyway slot 5mm wide (Y) and 2.5mm deep (radial, in X) is cut along the bore wall on the +X side, running the full 60mm length. The keyway is centered at X = (bore radius - half keyway depth) = 5.75mm from the origin.
primitive_plan_summary: Flanged shaft coupling with two disc flanges and a central hub cylinder, featuring a center bore, keyway, and six bolt holes through the flanges.

verifier_score.feedback: All constraints met. The model correctly implements all specified features: (1) Bounding box is 50×50×60mm matching the flange diameter and total length. (2) Three coaxial cylindrical sections are visible - bottom flange, central hub, and top flange with correct proportions. (3) The center bore (14mm diameter) is clearly visible in the high-angle rear view. (4) Six bolt holes are visible and correctly spaced at 60° intervals on the 38mm PCD - they pass through the flanges only (since the hub at 28mm diameter is narrower than the 38mm PCD, the bolt holes only intersect the flange material). (5) The keyway slot is visible on the +X side of the bore in the top-down view. (6) Volume of ~50,636 mm³ is physically plausible for this geometry. (7) The model is valid, watertight, with no self-intersections. (8) Center of mass is nearly at origin in X/Y and exactly at Z=30mm (midpoint), confirming symmetry. The slight X offset (-0.006mm) is consistent with the keyway being on the +X side.
